{
  "name": "Template Method",
  "seminal_paper": {
    "arxiv_id": "1106.1842",
    "doi": "10.1016/j.jcta.2012.01.006",
    "citation": "J. D. Currie and N. Rampersad, Fixed points avoiding Abelian k-powers, J. Combin. Theory Ser. A 119(5): 942-948, 2012.",
    "year": 2012
  },
  "home_domain": "combinatorics_on_words",
  "what_it_does": "The Template Method gives finite certificates for avoiding Abelian k-powers in morphic fixed points. It encodes candidate repetitions as templates with Parikh-vector offsets and relies on a contraction condition for the morphism's incidence matrix.",
  "input_requirements": [
    "a finite alphabet Sigma and an infinite word that is a fixed point h^omega(a) of a morphism h : Sigma* -> Sigma*",
    "a forbidden Abelian k-power expressed as the zero-offset k-template using Parikh vectors over Sigma",
    "an invertible incidence matrix M_h with contracting inverse in the Currie-Rampersad norm",
    "an effective finite check for which bounded templates occur as factors of h^omega(a)"
  ],
  "output_signature": "a finite decision certificate that a given morphic fixed point avoids the specified Abelian k-power",
  "input_match": [
    {
      "requirement": "a finite alphabet Sigma and an infinite word that is a fixed point h^omega(a) of a morphism h : Sigma* -> Sigma*",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "A4 obstruction states that the powerful numbers are not generated by a finite-state morphism or substitution rule."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "a forbidden Abelian k-power expressed as the zero-offset k-template using Parikh vectors over Sigma",
      "match": "PARTIAL",
      "evidence": "Set.IsAPOfLength captures the numeric 3-term progression condition, but not a Parikh-vector Abelian-power template."
    },
    {
      "requirement": "an invertible incidence matrix M_h with contracting inverse in the Currie-Rampersad norm",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match"
    },
    {
      "requirement": "an effective finite check for which bounded templates occur as factors of h^omega(a)",
      "match": "NO",
      "evidence": "no match"
    }
  ],
  "known_obstructions": [
    "The sequence of powerful numbers is an ordered numeric sequence, not a finite-alphabet morphic fixed point.",
    "A three-term progression of consecutive powerful numbers is a condition on unbounded numeric gaps, not on Parikh-vector equality of finite word blocks.",
    "The consecutiveness condition depends on the global ordering of powerful numbers, while the Template Method is local-factor based.",
    "The required incidence matrix and contraction hypothesis have no evident analogue for powerful numbers."
  ],
  "fit_score": 0.125,
  "source_analog_id": "A4"
}
